title: lithocarpus rhabdostachyus subsp. eurhabdostachyus
intro: Lithocarpus rhabdostachyus subsp. eurhabdostachyus, commonly known as the Taiwan cork tree, is a species of evergreen tree belonging to the beech family (Fagaceae). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains triterpenoid saponins that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The medicinal properties of Lithocarpus rhabdostachyus subsp. eurhabdostachyus have been found to provide various health benefits, including the relief of fever, headache, and gastrointestinal issues, as well as the improvement of immune function and wound healing. The plant is traditionally used in herbal preparations, such as teas, decoctions, and infusions, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
